title: Slow Cooked Middle Eastern Sauerkraut Tagine
description: A winey, gooey, and cozy Middle Eastern dish featuring white sauerkraut and beurre-monte, friendly to Buddhist temple dietary preferences. This dish is slow cooked to perfection in a tagine.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Stir in the ground cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the drained and rinsed white sauerkraut, chopped dried apricots, and pitted prunes, and stir to combine.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 hours, or until the sauerkraut is tender and the flavors have melded together.
Stir in the beurre-monte and continue to simmer for an additional 30 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and the flavors have intensified.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
